summary: Practices llamar and the reflexive me llamo for introductions, reviews imperfect -ía for -er/-ir verbs with tener, and introduces the three irregular verbs in the imperfect: ver (veía), ser (era), and ir (iba). Also teaches iba a + infinitive for 'was going to'.

teachings.summary: Reviewed llamar (to call) and the reflexive structure me llamo (I call myself) for introducing oneself, plus ¿Cómo te llamas? and ¿Cómo se llama?
teachings.notes: Double l (ll) creates a /yuh/ sound in most Spanish dialects.

teachings.summary: Reviewed how English -tain verbs convert to -tener in Spanish: contain=contener, obtain=obtener, maintain=mantener, sustain=sostener.
teachings.notes: Mantener: man- is from mano (hand). Sostener changed slightly from sus- to sos-.

teachings.summary: Practiced imperfect with tener (tenía) and combined with tener que for 'had to'. Even though English uses 'had to' (not 'was having to'), the imperfect works fine in Spanish.

examples.english: I used to have a big house
We used to have a big house

examples.spanish: Tenía cuatro, pero ahora no me queda
examples.english: I used to have four, but now I don't have any left
I had to see you yesterday
Didn't you have to do it yesterday?

teachings.notes: The accent stays in the same place on -ía: tenía, teníamos.

teachings.summary: Ver is irregular in the imperfect: veía (not vía). The same extra e irregularity from the present (veo) carries into the imperfect.

examples.english: I was seeing / I used to see

teachings.notes: Same extra e as in veo (present). Only three verbs are irregular in the imperfect.

teachings.summary: Ser in the imperfect uses era as the base. Easy to remember because 'an era' in English is a line in time in the past.

examples.english: I used to be a student

teachings.notes: Mnemonic: 'era' in English means a line in time in the past, just like the imperfect. No word for 'a' needed before professions: Era estudiante.

teachings.summary: Ir in the imperfect is iba - a blend of the -aba and -ía patterns. Very useful for 'was going to' + infinitive structures.

examples.english: I was going / He was going
I was going to call you
I was going to do it
We were going to make them

teachings.notes: Iba blends -aba and -ía. Don't forget the 'a' connector: iba A hacerlo (ir is an 'a' verb).
